behavioral_changes.summary: sys_run_metrics now carries three per-run refresh-policy fraction columns (`fraction_full`, `fraction_incremental`, `fraction_no_refresh`) through the table definition, intermediate row DTO, and generated PySpark schema/data row.
Run metrics collection now computes refresh-policy fractions from terminal nodes only (`Completed`/`Failed`) and matches recognized refresh-policy names case-insensitively.
Null/empty/unrecognized refresh policies and non-terminal nodes are excluded from the fraction denominator so recognized fractions stay normalized.
Fraction computation now leaves zeros when `nodeMetrics` is null or when no eligible terminal nodes exist, avoiding divide-by-zero on empty eligible sets.

Generated PySpark literals for double-valued fraction fields are now emitted as `float(...)` with round-trip, invariant-culture formatting so DoubleType rows are accepted and fraction precision is preserved.
The summary query now aggregates `fraction_full` with null-coalescing and maps it to `/summary.refreshPolicyDistribution.full`, rounded to 1 decimal place.
The summary query now aggregates `fraction_incremental` with null-coalescing and maps it to `/summary.refreshPolicyDistribution.incremental`, rounded to 1 decimal place.
The summary query now aggregates `fraction_no_refresh` with null-coalescing and maps it to `/summary.refreshPolicyDistribution.noRefresh`, rounded to 1 decimal place.
`InsightsSummaryResponse` now always exposes a non-null `refreshPolicyDistribution` container.

The added documentation defines a contract that each eligible run contributes exactly 1.0 across the three refresh buckets, and the summed distribution equals the number of runs with at least one refresh-eligible node.

grounding_evidence.reason: Adds persisted sys_run_metrics columns for per-run refresh-policy fractions.
Contract-bearing comment defining eligible-node scope, exclusion rules, and the sum-to-1 invariant for fraction computation.
Implements the nodeMetrics null guard, terminal-node filter, recognized-policy allowlist, and case-insensitive refresh-policy matching.
Guards against zero denominators and computes normalized per-run fraction values for the three policy buckets.
Adds the three fraction columns to the generated PySpark schema.
Emits the three fraction values into the generated PySpark merge row via the new double-formatting helper.
Defines the float-wrapper regression fix and the round-trip/invariant-culture formatting contract for generated Python double literals.
Adds the three fraction properties to the intermediate RunMetricsRow data contract.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: SUM(CASE WHEN run_date >= @startDate THEN COALESCE(fraction_full, 0) ELSE 0 END) AS fractionFullSum,
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the SQL projection for aggregated full-refresh fractions and coalesces legacy NULL values to 0.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: SUM(CASE WHEN run_date >= @startDate THEN COALESCE(fraction_incremental, 0) ELSE 0 END) AS fractionIncrementalSum,
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the SQL projection for aggregated incremental-refresh fractions and coalesces legacy NULL values to 0.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: SUM(CASE WHEN run_date >= @startDate THEN COALESCE(fraction_no_refresh, 0) ELSE 0 END) AS fractionNoRefreshSum,
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the SQL projection for aggregated no-refresh fractions and coalesces legacy NULL values to 0.
Adds the new response property and default-initializes it so callers receive a non-null distribution container.
Contract-bearing comment documenting the aggregate semantics and total-sum guarantee of the new response object.
Adds the `full` field to the `refreshPolicyDistribution` response shape.
Adds the `incremental` field to the `refreshPolicyDistribution` response shape.
Adds the `noRefresh` field to the `refreshPolicyDistribution` response shape.
Maps aggregated full-refresh fractions into the response, defaulting DB NULL to 0 and rounding to 1 decimal place.
Maps aggregated incremental-refresh fractions into the response, defaulting DB NULL to 0 and rounding to 1 decimal place.
Maps aggregated no-refresh fractions into the response, defaulting DB NULL to 0 and rounding to 1 decimal place.

scenario_sketches.title: Compute normalized per-run fractions from mixed terminal refresh policies with case-insensitive matching
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new computation block accepts recognized Full/Incremental/NoRefresh policy strings case-insensitively and distributes a run's total 1.0 across the three buckets using eligible terminal nodes.

scenario_sketches.title: Exclude skipped/cancelled and null-empty-unrecognized refresh policies from the denominator
scenario_sketches.rationale: The added filter explicitly narrows eligibility to Completed/Failed nodes with recognized policies; non-terminal nodes and missing/unknown policy values should not contribute to either numerator or denominator.

scenario_sketches.title: Leave all fraction fields at zero when nodeMetrics is null
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new outer `if (nodeMetrics != null)` is a separately revertable null guard that should preserve default zero fractions instead of reading node data.

scenario_sketches.title: Leave all fraction fields at zero when no eligible terminal nodes exist
scenario_sketches.rationale: The `denominator > 0` branch is a distinct divide-by-zero guard; a run with zero eligible nodes should keep all fractions at 0 rather than attempting a division.

scenario_sketches.title: Propagate aggregated full-refresh fractions into refreshPolicyDistribution.full
scenario_sketches.rationale: `fraction_full` is added through storage, PySpark schema/data emission, SQL aggregation, response model, and reader mapping, so one positive-flow test should prove the `full` value survives end-to-end.

scenario_sketches.title: Default refreshPolicyDistribution.full to 0 when legacy full-fraction data is NULL
scenario_sketches.rationale: The COALESCE/IsDBNull handling for `fraction_full` is independently revertable from the field addition itself and should protect callers from legacy NULL data.

scenario_sketches.title: Propagate aggregated incremental-refresh fractions into refreshPolicyDistribution.incremental
scenario_sketches.rationale: `fraction_incremental` is a separate added field/column and deserves its own end-to-end contract from persisted run metric to summary payload.

scenario_sketches.title: Default refreshPolicyDistribution.incremental to 0 when legacy incremental-fraction data is NULL
scenario_sketches.rationale: The null guard for `fraction_incremental` is a distinct defensive invariant for a separate input column and should be locked independently.

scenario_sketches.title: Propagate aggregated no-refresh fractions into refreshPolicyDistribution.noRefresh
scenario_sketches.rationale: `fraction_no_refresh` is a third distinct added field/column and needs its own positive-flow contract through query and response mapping.

scenario_sketches.title: Default refreshPolicyDistribution.noRefresh to 0 when legacy no-refresh data is NULL
scenario_sketches.rationale: The null guard for `fraction_no_refresh` protects a separate input and is independently revertable from the other two COALESCE/DBNull guards.

scenario_sketches.title: Materialize a non-null refreshPolicyDistribution container even when the summary has no data row
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new response property is default-initialized at the model layer, so no-row/default paths should still return a non-null object for callers to read safely.

scenario_sketches.title: Wrap zero and integer-valued doubles in Python float() so DoubleType rows are not dropped
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new helper comment documents a prior row-loss bug for values like 0 and 1; a regression test should lock in the `float(...)` wrapper for those exact cases.

scenario_sketches.title: Emit non-integer fraction literals with round-trip precision and invariant-culture decimal points
scenario_sketches.rationale: The helper's `R` formatting and invariant culture are separate from the float-wrapper fix and should preserve parsable precision and `.` decimal separators for fractional values.

scenario_sketches.title: Enforce the documented contract that full+incremental+noRefresh sums to eligible-run count
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new comments define an externally meaningful invariant across runs: each eligible run contributes exactly 1.0 across the three buckets, so the summed distribution should equal the number of runs with at least one refresh-eligible node.
